channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Ancient ocean may have shaped Central Asia's mountains
item.description: Scientists have found evidence that the vanished Tethys Ocean may have helped form Central Asia's mountainous landscape during the dinosaur era. Using...

item.title: Study may explain why most humans are right-handed
item.description: A new study suggests humans became predominantly right-handed due to two key evolutionary changes: walking upright and developing larger...

item.title: Plant thought extinct for 60 years rediscovered in Australia
item.description: A chance photo in the Australian outback led to the rediscovery of a plant believed extinct for nearly 60 years, showing that ordinary people can make significant scientific contributions.

item.title: Schrödinger's clock: Time could tick faster and slower simultaneously
item.description: Time may be stranger than Einstein thought. Physicists are exploring the possibility that a single clock could exist in a quantum superposition, ticking at different rates at once.
